task: I need a simple way to keep a job from bleeding money when there are delays, change orders, and a couple of vendors sending bills at different times. What should I be watching so I don’t miss anything that could come back on us later?

turns.agent_response:
Watch these first, in this order:

1. **Change order status**
   - What changed
   - Who asked for it
   - Whether it’s **approved in writing**
   - If it’s not signed, treat it as **at risk**

2. **Delay causes**
   - Weather, owner, inspection, vendor, crew, design, access
   - Note **who caused the delay**
   - Note whether it affects **time, cost, or both**

3. **Vendor bill timing**
   - Match every bill to the **PO / quote / approved change**
   - Watch for bills that show up **before the work is fully approved**
   - Flag anything with missing backup right away

4. **Extra work done without notice**
   - Work started before approval
   - Materials ordered off the record
   - Crew time spent on “small favors” that weren’t captured

5. **Hold points**
   - Inspection not cleared
   - Delivery not confirmed
   - Work that’s about to get covered up before it’s verified

### Simple rule
If it affects **scope, time, or money**, it gets logged the same day.

### Quick daily check
At the end of the day, ask:
- What changed today?
- What is still unapproved?
- What was delayed, and why?
- What bill or cost might show up later?
- What needs written backup before tomorrow?

### What to keep in one place
Use one sheet with these columns:
- **Item**
- **Date**
- **Cause**
- **Cost impact**
- **Time impact**
- **Approved?**
- **Who owes the next step**
- **Backup attached?**

If you want, I can turn this into a **one-page job cost watch list** you can use on site.

user_simulator.text:
Close, but I want it tighter.

You gave me the right buckets. What I need is the **bare-minimum control list** I can actually run on a job without babysitting it all day.

Watch this:
- **Change order log** — request date, who asked, cost, time, signed or not
- **Delay log** — what stopped it, who caused it, days lost, dollar hit if any
- **Vendor billing** — invoice date, what it covers, matched to PO/quote/change order, paid or not
- **Outstanding approvals** — anything verbal, anything started but not signed, anything waiting on owner/inspectors
- **Backcharge / claim risk** — damage, rework, standby, extra labor, rental idle time
- **Daily backup** — photos, emails, texts, inspection notes, delivery slips

What I don’t want is a pretty worksheet that nobody uses. I want the stuff that protects me when the owner starts squawking and the accountant asks why the margin slipped.

So give me this next:
**a one-page daily tracking sheet with the exact fields, in order, no fluff.**

turns.agent_response:
Here’s the **bare-minimum one-page daily tracking sheet** in the order I’d run it.

## Daily Job Cost / Risk Log

### 1) Change Orders
- Date requested
- Requested by
- Description of change
- Cost impact
- Time impact
- Signed? (Y/N)
- Status: verbal / pending / approved / rejected

### 2) Delays
- Date
- What stopped the work
- Who caused it
- Days lost
- Cost impact
- Time impact
- Notes / backup

### 3) Vendor Billing
- Invoice date
- Vendor name
- What it covers
- Amount
- PO / quote / change order matched? (Y/N)
- Approved? (Y/N)
- Paid? (Y/N)
- Missing backup? (Y/N)

### 4) Outstanding Approvals
- Item
- Who is holding it
- What’s missing
- Date sent
- Next follow-up date
- Risk if not approved

### 5) Backcharge / Claim Risk
- Date
- Issue
- Who caused it
- Rework / damage / standby / idle time
- Dollar risk
- Backup attached? (Y/N)
- Sent to owner / vendor / insurer? (Y/N)

### 6) Daily Backup
- Photos taken? (Y/N)
- Emails saved? (Y/N)
- Texts saved? (Y/N)
- Inspection notes saved? (Y/N)
- Delivery slips saved? (Y/N)

## End-of-day check
- What changed today?
- What is still unapproved?
- What bill came in?
- What delay hit us?
- What needs backup tomorrow?

## Simple cutoff
If it affects **scope, time, or money**, it gets logged the same day.

If you want, I can turn this into a **clipboard-ready table** with blank lines you can print and fill out fast.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah. That’s the list.

Keep it on one page, no extras:

1. Change orders
2. Delays
3. Vendor billing
4. Outstanding approvals
5. Backcharge / claim risk
6. Daily backup

That’s enough to catch the bleed before it turns into a fight later. The end-of-day check is right too.

Clipboard-ready table would be useful, but this answers what I asked.
